@Peluolaide If that level of institutional adoption is real, it’s a major validation point for Pyth Network.

What to watch next: how fast Pyth onboards additional publishers, whether major DeFi protocols switch from aggregated feeds to first party institutional data, and if Bloomberg or Refinitiv respond with their own on chain offerings.

The incumbents have not moved yet. That window is closing.

Follow @fiftyonexyz for institutional digital asset intelligence.

The competitive signal: Chainlink built its oracle business on node operator aggregation. Pyth is betting that first party institutional data wins in the long run.

When Fidelity publishes its own price feeds directly, the middleman layer becomes less relevant.

Here is what happened. Pyth Network signed six major financial institutions to publish FX, equities, fixed income, and precious metals data directly on chain.

Not aggregated. Not scraped. First party data from the institutions that generate it.

BREAKING: Fidelity, Euronext, and Tradeweb are now publishing proprietary financial data directly on chain.

Pyth Network just launched a Data Marketplace that puts institutional market data on 100+ blockchains. The $50 billion market data industry just got disrupted. https://t.co/zFoNB9EZkE

Let’s be honest: this isn’t an upgrade.
It’s an indictment.
The old model took truth, repackaged it, and sold it back.

Pyth Data Marketplace flips it;
• Data comes from the source
• Institutions keep control, pricing, attribution
• Global access, no middlemen.

Using the old model: institutions create data → vendors gate it → markets rent access.

The new model by @PythNetwork features direct, programmable distribution.
